issues.detail: All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms like 'The', 'food', 'must', 'be', 'dry', 'surface', 'added', 'fat', 'splatter' are English words. Should annotate German content words: Gargut, Oberfläche, trocken, Fett, spritzt.
gloss_hint 'spray' on 'Spritz' is wrong sense — in this context spritzen means 'ejaculate' (sense 7), not 'spray' (sense 1).

Translation is wrong: 'More and more developers are working on the software, rather than full-time developers employed by large companies' — the original says 'No longer hobby programmers work on the software, but rather permanently employed developers from predominantly large companies'. The meaning is inverted. Also missing annotation for 'angestellte' (the target word) and 'Hobbyprogrammierer', 'Entwickler', 'Software', 'Firmen'.

Translation inverts the meaning. Original: 'Nicht mehr Hobbyprogrammierer arbeiten an der Software, sondern fest angestellte Entwickler überwiegend großer Firmen' = 'No longer hobby programmers work on the software, but rather permanently employed developers predominantly from large companies like Microsoft, Google, or IBM.'
Annotation for 'anzustellen' has wrong lemma 'ankündigen' (announce) — should be 'anstellen' (the actual verb).

Translation is incomplete — only covers the first half. Full text has two speakers: 'Phew, no one can stand that smell!' — 'Oh come on, don't be such a baby!' The second part (the anstellen usage) is cut off. Also annotations only cover the first sentence, missing the key 'stell dich nicht so an' part.
Translation only covers first half of the dialogue. Missing: '– Ach komm, stell dich nicht so an!' = '– Oh come on, stop making such a fuss!'

Translation only covers second half of dialogue, missing first part. Also: 'stellt' lemma is 'stellen' but should be 'anstellen' (separable verb sich anstellen). 'bloß' is used as particle/adverb here meaning 'just/only', not adjective 'bare'.
Missing first part of dialogue: '"That was a nasty foul!" – "Oh nonsense, he's just making a fuss!"'
All annotations are on English translation text ('expression', 'correct', 'always', 'and', 'called', 'a', 'tautology') instead of German source words (Ausdruck, richtig, immer, Tautologie, genannt).

All annotations are on English translation text ('expression', 'be', 'never', 'executed', 'because', 'it', 'is', 'dead', 'code', 'reached') instead of German source words (Ausdruck, ausgeführt, toter, Kode, erreicht).

gloss_hint 'expression (phrase)' on 'Ausdruck' is wrong sense — this example is about 'artistic style' (sense 4), not 'expression (phrase)' (sense 0). Also 'jedermanns' lemma 'jeder Mann' is wrong — should be 'jedermann'.

Multiple wrong gloss_hints: 'Kunstfliegen' has hint 'expression (phrase)' — Kunstfliegen is aerobatics, not related to Ausdruck at all. 'Sehnsucht' has hint 'expression (phrase)' — Sehnsucht means longing/yearning. 'Königsdisziplin' has hint 'artistic style' — Königsdisziplin means supreme discipline/crown jewel. 'Ausdruck' gloss_hint 'expression (phrase)' wrong — in this context it means 'symbol/sign' (sense 8).
'Grün' annotated as adjective with lemma 'grün' — but in this sentence 'die Farbe Grün' uses Grün as a noun (the color green). Should be pos: noun, lemma: Grün.
'Ich' has wrong gloss_hint 'we' — 'Ich' means 'I'. Also 'Grün' has gloss_hint 'green (color)' but in context (plucking at the greenery) it means 'greenery' (sense 3), not the color.
'Grün' lemma is 'grün' (adjective) but here 'das Grün der Landschaft' is the noun Grün meaning greenery. Should be lemma: Grün, pos: noun.

'Grün' annotated as adjective lemma 'grün' but here 'Grün ist Trumpf' uses Grün as a noun (the card suit). Should be pos: noun, lemma: Grün. Also 'Trumpf' lemma is lowercase 'trumpf' — should be 'Trumpf'.

'Stich' gloss_hint is 'sting' but this example is sense 0 'stab (weapon)' — 'Der Stich ging tief unter die Haut' is about a stab wound going deep, not an insect sting. Translation 'The sting went deep under the skin' should be 'The stab went deep under the skin'.
'The sting went deep under the skin' — should be 'The stab went deep under the skin' (this is about a weapon wound, not an insect sting).

Annotations use English lemmas: 'Stich' lemma is 'sting', 'Wunde' lemma is 'wound'. Should be German: Stich, Wunde. Duplicate 'weiter' annotation. Also translation says 'sting' but given sense 0 context (wound festering), 'stab' is more appropriate.
Translation uses 'sketches' but 'Stiche' in this context means 'engravings' (sense 2). Missing annotation for 'Stiche'.
'sketches' should be 'engravings' — Stiche here refers to printed engravings of military campaigns.
'Stichen' gloss_hint is 'stamp' — should be 'engraving'. The Stiche here refer to the engraving printing technique used for stamps, not the stamps themselves.

All annotations use English lemmas: 'Eitelkeit' → 'vanity', 'der' → 'the', 'Teufel' → 'Satan', 'Stich' → 'sting'. Should use German lemmas: Eitelkeit, der, Teufel, Stich. Also 'Stich' in card game context means 'trick' (sense 3), not 'sting'. Translation 'dealt a blow' is questionable — 'einen Stich gemacht' means 'won a trick' in card game context.
'Vanity has won; the devil has dealt a blow' — in card game context 'einen Stich machen' means 'to take a trick'. Better: 'Vanity has won; the devil has taken a trick.'
Annotations use English lemmas: 'Grau' → 'gray', 'Stich' → 'streak'. Should be German: Grau, Stich.
'Stich' gloss_hint is 'sting' but in this context 'einen Stich haben' (milk) means it has turned sour — sense 7 'tartar (food)', a sour aftertaste.
'Oh, the milk has a sting!' is not idiomatic. Should be 'Ugh, the milk has gone off!' or 'Ugh, the milk has turned sour!'
'Dieb' gloss_hint is 'thief' but this example illustrates sense 1 'rascal' — the 'kleine Dieb' is an endearing term for a child, matching the humorous/outdated sense.
'machen' lemma for 'machen...aus' — this is separable verb 'ausmachen', not 'machen'. The lemma should be 'ausmachen'.
'macht' lemma is 'machen' with gloss_hint 'power' — should be lemma 'ausmachen' (separable verb 'macht...aus'). gloss_hint 'power' makes no sense.
'Morgen' lemma is English 'morning' — should be German 'Morgen'. 'Kranichen' gloss_hint 'bird migration' is wrong — Kranich means crane (the bird).

All annotations use English lemmas: 'Haus' → 'house', 'junges' → 'young', 'Mädchen' → 'girl', 'Unkraut' → 'weed'. Should be German: Haus, jung, Mädchen, Unkraut. Missing annotation for 'machte...aus' (ausmachen).
'ausgemacht' gloss_hint is 'identify' which is acceptable but 'spot' matches the word file gloss_en better.
'machten' lemma is 'machen' but this is separable verb 'ausmachen' ('machten...aus'). Lemma should be 'ausmachen'.
'When you agree with a jack, you say Maumau' — 'agree' is wrong sense. 'ausmachen' here means 'finish/end the game'. Better: 'When you finish with a jack, you say Mau-Mau.'
'Vorlage' gloss_hint is 'template' but in this context it means 'draft' (sense 5) — a legislative draft being put to a vote.
'Vorlage' gloss_hint is 'template' but in this context it means 'forward shift' (sense 9) — body weight leaning forward in skiing.
First annotation has English form 'word' — this is an English annotation on the translation, not the German text. Should annotate 'Wort' from the German source.

All annotations use English lemmas: 'Deutsche' → 'German', 'Wortschatz' → 'vocabulary', 'geben' → 'exist', 'dafür' → 'for it', 'dass' → 'that', 'es' → 'it', 'längere' → 'longer', 'Zusammensetzungen' → 'compound words', 'kann' → 'can', 'das' → 'the', 'Wort' → 'word'. All should use German lemmas.
'Zusammensetzung' gloss_hint lists all senses piped — should be 'composition' for this context (the composition of active ingredients).
'Wurzel' gloss_hint is 'root' but this example is about sense 7 'etymon' — a Hebrew root from which Yiddish verbs are formed (historical/linguistic root, not morphological).
'Vorlage' gloss_hint is 'template' but in this context it means 'original' (sense 1) — the original work used as model for a drawing exercise.
